classifications.phrase: Inflation has fallen substantially since the peak in 2022
Both headline and trimmed mean inflation were within the 2–3 per cent range
inflation in the September quarter may be higher than expected
private demand is recovering a little more rapidly than expected
private consumption is picking up as real household incomes rise
labour market conditions have been broadly steady in recent months and remain a little tight
wages growth has eased from its peak
growth in unit labour costs remains high
stronger-than-expected data on growth and inflation
indications that inflation may be persistent in some areas
the Board decided that it was appropriate to maintain the cash rate

payload.rationale: The RBA signals a cautious hold stance, balancing dovish inflation progress against hawkish concerns about persistent inflation, strong demand, and tight labour markets.
